pages.raw_text:
Some trouble is being expert
enced by Comptroller Farley with
the Graves street railway people
who have failed to submit a re-
port of their car mileage and to
pay pro rata to the city as agreed
when the franchises for the various
lines were granted. The sum to be
paid is 2 mills for each car mile.
Just what the sum will amount to
is unknown.


Some time ago the Traction Of
ficers promised to have a state
ment ready by. the first of the year
but have failed to turn it in. Lately
the excuse was made that the fig
ures were being subjected to COM
parison to insure accuracy.


The Washington Water Power
has paid up and the sum is only
$509.14, very cheap for the million
dollar franchise granted. For the
lighting plant $4,000 a year is paid,
and this was also recently turned
in.


Several months ago, at Mayor
MOores request, the comptroller
prepared a list of the franchises
existing and the amounts they
were paying or owed the city. 1t
was this statement which revealed
the fact that the Traction people
are one year in arrears.
